definitions.text: The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. The smallest discrete unit of spoken language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more phonemes and one or more morphemes The smallest discrete unit of written language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more letters or symbols and one or more morphemes A discrete, meaningful unit of language approved by an authority or native speaker (compare non-word). A sign used in a sign language.
The smallest discrete unit of spoken language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more phonemes and one or more morphemes
To say or write (something) using particular words; to phrase (something).
examples: I’m not sure how to word this letter to the council.

definitions.text: To flatter with words, to cajole.
To ply or overpower with words.
Truth, indeed, that is the truth! The shortened form of the statement "My word is my bond."
examples: "Yo, that movie was epic!" / "Word?" ("You speak the truth?") / "Word." ("I speak the truth.")

definitions.text: An abbreviated form of word up; a statement of the acknowledgment of fact with a hint of nonchalant approval.
